title: Poa brownii
taxonomy: Poa brownii is a species of grass in the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses or cereals. It is classified within the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. Poa brownii is native to New Zealand and is found in a variety of habitats, including grasslands and mountainous regions. The classification of Poa brownii is also reflected in its botanical name, which is derived from the Latin words for "meadow" and "brown", respectively. Poa brownii is often considered a distinct species within the Poa genus due to its unique characteristics and adaptations.
